Protection and recovery pattern: Time travel/fail-safe for logical errors; snapshots/clones for controlled points; cross-region dataset replication or Managed DR for regional outage
Critical caveat: Validate replication lag and compute readiness; failover is a controlled business action

Protection and recovery pattern: Soft delete, Object Versioning where required, retention policies, dual-region/multi-region, turbo replication for selected Tier 0/1 objects
Critical caveat: Protection controls do not replace independent cyber-recovery copies for all threats

Protection and recovery pattern: Subscription/topic retention, snapshots and seek; recreate regional subscriptions/topics from IaC
Critical caveat: Replay can create duplicates; consumers must be idempotent and sequence-aware

Protection and recovery pattern: Recreate jobs from immutable template/artifact; use source checkpoints, Pub/Sub replay, drain/cancel controls and snapshots where supported
Critical caveat: Dataflow jobs are regional; recovery is pipeline-specific, not an automatic cross-region failover

Protection and recovery pattern: Resume after regional recovery; stream recovery for source failover/log gaps; recreate in paired region if required
Critical caveat: Regional outage pauses streams; destination may contain duplicates after recovery

Protection and recovery pattern: Scheduled environment snapshots in protected cross-region bucket; restore or rebuild paired environment
Critical caveat: Snapshot contains Airflow database and fernet key; protect snapshot and externalize secrets

Protection and recovery pattern: Automatic/manual backup for same-instance restore; import/export plus Git-managed LookML for cross-instance recovery
Critical caveat: Same-instance backup alone is not cross-region recovery; test instance recreation and content migration

Protection and recovery pattern: Reproducible training artifacts, model versions, prompt/config source, regional endpoint deployment manifests and application routing
Critical caveat: Capabilities and locations vary; validate supported regions, quotas and data residency

Protection and recovery pattern: Authoritative metadata definitions, glossary and policies in versioned repositories or supported exports; rebuild integration/configuration
Critical caveat: Do not assume every managed metadata object has native cross-region backup

Protection and recovery pattern: Health-checked failover/geolocation policies where supported; manual override path
Critical caveat: DNS is not data replication; TTL and client caching affect observed RTO

Protection and recovery pattern: Versioning/soft delete, retention, restricted IAM, alternate recovery bucket or export and tested restore
Critical caveat: State restore requires serial integrity, lock control and reconciliation with real resources
